rationale: The citing paper uses Dillon et al. (2001) as the source of the "general brand impression" (GBI) construct and makes that latent construct central to its model of underlying brand sentiment. Dillon et al. is cited in model development to justify decomposing observed sentiment into a brand-level latent component plus topic-specific components, and later to support expected topic effects on expressed sentiment. This is more than background listing because the cited concept directly structures how the core latent variable is defined and interpreted.
relation_description: The citing study applies Dillon et al.'s GBI idea to social media listening by treating GBI as the latent brand-sentiment factor that links sentiment expression and venue choice. It transfers that conceptual decomposition logic into a new empirical setting rather than reusing Dillon et al.'s original estimation framework.
